title_id: Kesalahan yang Harus Dihindari dalam Penafsiran Al-Quran
content_en:
Ibn Taymiyyah's closing chapter is a masterclass in identifying and categorizing the errors that have historically marred Quranic interpretation, presented not as abstract taxonomy but as practical guidance for the student who wishes to approach the Quran with genuine fidelity. The errors he identifies span a wide range — linguistic, methodological, theological, and sectarian — and his analysis remains strikingly relevant in an age when Quranic interpretation has proliferated beyond any institutional framework of quality control.

The first category of error is interpreting the Quran without sufficient knowledge of classical Arabic. Ibn Taymiyyah documents cases where misreadings of grammatical structure, failure to recognize idiomatic expressions, or ignorance of the semantic range of key vocabulary led interpreters to positions inconsistent with the text's actual meaning. This error is particularly dangerous because it presents itself in the guise of Quranic authority, making the interpreter's own linguistic deficiency appear as divine guidance.

The second category is interpreting the Quran by isolated opinion (ra'y mujarrad) divorced from the transmitted tradition. This error is committed by those who approach the text with a predetermined conclusion — theological, political, or philosophical — and read the text through that lens. The Quran becomes a quarry from which materials are extracted to build whatever edifice the interpreter has already decided to construct. Ibn Taymiyyah is particularly alert to this error because it was widespread among the theological schools of his time, each of which had characteristic interpretive tendencies shaped by their prior doctrinal commitments.

The third error is the excessive use of allegorical interpretation (ta'wil) to strip passages of their apparent meaning. Ibn Taymiyyah is the preeminent classical defender of the Athari or textualist position in matters of Allah's attributes: when the Quran describes Allah as having a hand, a face, or being above the throne, these descriptions are to be accepted as true without asking how, rather than interpreted as metaphors for abstract attributes. His criticism of the Mu'tazilite and certain Ash'arite interpretive tendencies on this point is one of the central polemical contributions of his treatise.

A fourth error is the misuse of the science of asbab al-nuzul (occasions of revelation). Some interpreters erroneously conclude that because a verse was revealed in response to a specific situation, its ruling applies only to that situation. This error produces an unduly restricted reading of Quranic commands. Ibn Taymiyyah establishes the classical principle: 'The ruling follows the generality of the expression, not the specificity of the occasion' — meaning that a verse revealed about a specific person or event typically establishes a general ruling applicable to all similar cases.

The chapter ends with a call to interpretive humility: the exegete who is honest about his uncertainties, who notes when the classical authorities differ and presents the range of scholarly opinion, and who distinguishes between what is established by multiple authoritative sources and what is his own inference serves the Quran and its readers far better than the confident innovator who presents his opinions as the definitive meaning of Allah's speech.
content_id:
Bab penutup Ibnu Taimiyyah adalah sebuah mahakarya dalam mengidentifikasi dan mengkategorikan kesalahan-kesalahan yang secara historis telah mengotori penafsiran Al-Quran, disajikan bukan sebagai taksonomi abstrak melainkan sebagai panduan praktis bagi pelajar yang ingin mendekati Al-Quran dengan kesetiaan yang tulus. Kesalahan-kesalahan yang beliau identifikasi mencakup rentang yang luas — linguistik, metodologis, teologis, dan sektarian — dan analisisnya tetap relevan secara mencolok di era ketika penafsiran Al-Quran telah berkembang di luar kerangka kelembagaan pengawasan kualitas apa pun.

Kategori pertama kesalahan adalah menafsirkan Al-Quran tanpa pengetahuan yang cukup tentang bahasa Arab klasik. Ibnu Taimiyyah mendokumentasikan kasus-kasus di mana kesalahan baca struktur gramatikal, kegagalan mengenali ekspresi idiomatik, atau ketidaktahuan tentang cakupan semantik kosakata kunci membawa para penafsir ke posisi yang tidak konsisten dengan makna aktual teks. Kesalahan ini sangat berbahaya karena ia menyamar dalam kedok otoritas Qurani, membuat kekurangan linguistik penafsir sendiri tampak sebagai bimbingan ilahi.

Kategori kedua adalah menafsirkan Al-Quran dengan pendapat yang terisolasi (ra'y mujarrad) yang terputus dari tradisi yang diriwayatkan. Kesalahan ini dilakukan oleh mereka yang mendekati teks dengan kesimpulan yang sudah ditentukan sebelumnya — teologis, politis, atau filosofis — dan membaca teks melalui lensa tersebut. Al-Quran menjadi tambang dari mana bahan-bahan diekstraksi untuk membangun bangunan apa pun yang sudah diputuskan oleh penafsir untuk dibangun. Ibnu Taimiyyah sangat waspada terhadap kesalahan ini karena tersebar luas di antara sekolah-sekolah teologi di masanya, yang masing-masing memiliki kecenderungan penafsiran karakteristik yang dibentuk oleh komitmen doktrin sebelumnya.

Kesalahan ketiga adalah penggunaan berlebihan penafsiran alegoris (ta'wil) untuk mengupas bagian-bagian dari makna zahirnya. Ibnu Taimiyyah adalah pembela klasik terkemuka dari posisi Athari atau teksualisme dalam masalah-masalah sifat-sifat Allah: ketika Al-Quran menggambarkan Allah memiliki tangan, wajah, atau berada di atas Arsy, deskripsi-deskripsi ini harus diterima sebagai kebenaran tanpa bertanya bagaimana caranya, bukan ditafsirkan sebagai metafora untuk sifat-sifat abstrak. Kritiknya terhadap kecenderungan penafsiran Mu'tazilah dan sebagian Asy'ariyyah dalam hal ini adalah salah satu kontribusi polemis sentral dari risalahnya.

Kesalahan keempat adalah penyalahgunaan ilmu asbabun nuzul (sebab-sebab turunnya wahyu). Beberapa penafsir secara keliru menyimpulkan bahwa karena sebuah ayat diturunkan sebagai respons terhadap situasi tertentu, hukumnya hanya berlaku untuk situasi itu saja. Kesalahan ini menghasilkan bacaan yang terlalu sempit atas perintah-perintah Quranic. Ibnu Taimiyyah menetapkan prinsip klasik: 'Hukum mengikuti keumuman lafal, bukan kekhususan sebab' — yang berarti ayat yang diturunkan tentang seseorang atau peristiwa tertentu biasanya menetapkan hukum umum yang berlaku untuk semua kasus yang serupa.

Bab ini berakhir dengan seruan kepada kerendahan hati dalam penafsiran: mufasir yang jujur tentang ketidakpastiannya, yang mencatat ketika para otoritas klasik berbeda dan menyajikan ragam pendapat ulama, dan yang membedakan antara apa yang ditetapkan oleh beberapa sumber otoritatif dan apa yang merupakan inferensinya sendiri, melayani Al-Quran dan para pembacanya jauh lebih baik daripada pembaharu yang percaya diri yang menyajikan pendapat-pendapatnya sebagai makna definitif dari firman Allah.
content_ar: ابن تيمية's closing الباب is a masterclass in identifying and categorizing the errors that have historically marred القرآني interpretation, presented not as abstract taxonomy but as practical الهداية for the student who wishes to approach the القرآن الكريم with genuine fidelity. The errors he identifies span a wide range — linguistic, methodological, theological, and sectarian — and his analysis remains strikingly relevant in an age عندما القرآني interpretation has proliferated beyond any institutional framework of quality control. The أولاً category of error is interpreting the القرآن الكريم without sufficient العلم of الكلاسيكي Arabic. ابن تيمية documents cases where misreadings of grammatical structure, الخسارة to recognize idiomatic expressions, or ignorance of the semantic range of الرئيسي vocabulary led interpreters to positions inconsistent with the text's actual meaning. This error is particularly dangerous لأن it presents itself in the guise of القرآني authority, making the interpreter's own linguistic deficiency appear as divine الهداية. The ثانياً category is interpreting the القرآن الكريم by isolated opinion (ra'y mujarrad) divorced from the transmitted التراث. This error is committed by those who approach the text with a predetermined conclusion — theological, political, or philosophical — and read the text through that lens. The القرآن الكريم becomes a quarry from which materials are extracted to build whatever edifice the interpreter has already decided to construct. ابن تيمية is particularly alert to this error لأن it was widespread among the theological schools of his time, each of which had characteristic interpretive tendencies shaped by their prior doctrinal commitments. The ثالثاً error is the excessive use of allegorical interpretation (ta'wil) to strip passages of their apparent meaning. ابن تيمية is the preeminent الكلاسيكي defender of the الأثري or textualist position in matters of الله's attributes: عندما the القرآن الكريم describes الله as having a hand, a face, or being above the throne, these descriptions are to be accepted as true without asking how, rather than interpreted as metaphors for abstract attributes. His criticism of the Mu'tazilite and certain Ash'arite interpretive tendencies on this point is one of the المحوري polemical contributions of his treatise. A رابعاً error is the misuse of the science of asbab al-nuzul (occasions of الوحي). Some interpreters erroneously conclude that لأن a الآية was revealed in response to a specific situation, its ruling applies only to that situation. This error produces an unduly restricted reading of القرآني commands. ابن تيمية establishes the الكلاسيكي principle: 'The ruling follows the generality of the expression, not the specificity of the occasion' — meaning that a الآية revealed about a specific person or event typically establishes a general ruling applicable to all similar cases.
